architecture_decisions.recommendation: Store health check results as timestamped JSON log files in a new `monitor/logs/` directory structure organized by date (YYYY-MM-DD). Each API entry will have its status, latency, SSL expiry, and HTTP response code recorded.
architecture_decisions.rationale: JSON provides structured, human-readable logs that can be easily processed by reporting scripts. Timestamped files enable historical analysis and trend monitoring while avoiding single-point failures. This approach aligns with the project's existing validation patterns.

architecture_decisions.recommendation: Use GitHub Actions scheduled workflows (cron syntax) to run health checks daily at off-peak hours (e.g., 2 AM UTC). Create a new workflow file `.github/workflows/health_checks.yml` that runs the monitoring script and stores artifacts.
architecture_decisions.rationale: GitHub Actions is already integrated into the project for validation workflows. Scheduled workflows provide free, reliable execution without requiring external infrastructure. This maintains consistency with existing CI patterns.

architecture_decisions.recommendation: Extend the existing rate limiting pattern from `scripts/validate/format.py` to include exponential backoff with jitter and request caching. Use a decorator-based approach to wrap API calls with configurable delays between requests to the same domain.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Prevents overwhelming API providers with concurrent requests and respects rate limits. Exponential backoff with jitter handles transient failures gracefully. Reusing existing patterns ensures consistency and reduces maintenance overhead.

architecture_decisions.recommendation: Add a status column to the main README table with shields.io badges indicating operational status (green=operational, yellow=degraded, red=down). Generate this dynamically by processing the latest health check results and updating the README via a script.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Shields.io badges are widely recognized and provide immediate visual feedback. Dynamic README updates ensure users see current status without manual intervention. This enhances the directory's reliability and user trust.

architecture_decisions.recommendation: Use GitHub Issues API to create automated issues for APIs that fail consecutive health checks (e.g., 3 failures in a row). Include relevant metadata (API name, failure reason, last successful check) in the issue body and label with 'monitoring-alert'.
architecture_decisions.rationale: GitHub Issues are already used for project management, providing a centralized location for maintainer attention. Automated issue creation with consistent formatting reduces manual triaging effort and ensures timely responses to API degradation.

tickets.title: Extend link validation with health check capabilities
tickets.description: Modify `scripts/validate/links.py` to perform HTTP/S health checks including response codes, latency measurement, and SSL certificate expiry validation. Integrate the rate limiting decorator from `scripts/validate/format.py` and add configuration for timeout and retry logic.

tickets.title: Create health monitoring module and logging structure
tickets.description: Create `scripts/monitor/health.py` that imports the enhanced validation functions and runs scheduled checks on all APIs. Implement JSON logging to `monitor/logs/YYYY-MM-DD.json` with structured results. Create the necessary directory structure.

tickets.title: Implement GitHub Actions workflow for scheduled monitoring
tickets.description: Create `.github/workflows/health_checks.yml` with a daily cron schedule to run the health monitoring script. Configure artifact upload for log files and set up proper Python environment using existing requirements.

tickets.title: Build reporting and alerting module
tickets.description: Create `scripts/monitor/report.py` that analyzes recent log files, identifies APIs with consecutive failures, and opens GitHub Issues using the API. Implement deduplication logic to avoid duplicate issues for the same API failure.

tickets.title: Update README generation with status badges
tickets.description: Modify the reporting script to generate status badges (using shields.io format) based on latest health check results. Update the main README.md table to include a 'Status' column with appropriate badges for each API entry.

tickets.title: Add monitoring test coverage
tickets.description: Create comprehensive tests for the new monitoring modules in `scripts/tests/test_monitor_health.py` and `scripts/tests/test_monitor_report.py`. Include unit tests for rate limiting, health check logic, and report generation.

tickets.title: Document monitoring system in contributing guide
tickets.description: Update `CONTRIBUTING.md` and `scripts/README.md` to document the new monitoring system, including how to interpret status badges, how health checks work, and how to troubleshoot false positives.

technology_choices.rationale: Already present in the project's requirements and used by existing validation scripts. Provides robust HTTP/S handling with timeout support, SSL verification, and session management.
Standard library components that provide SSL certificate parsing and expiry validation without additional dependencies. Allows checking certificate validity dates programmatically.
Well-maintained Python wrapper for GitHub API v3 that simplifies authentication and issue creation. Reduces boilerplate code compared to direct REST API calls.
Already integrated into the project's CI/CD pipeline. Provides free, reliable scheduling with minimal configuration and good observability through the GitHub interface.
